reasoning: A had the most genuinely distinct set: the Debt creditor, the two-body Negotiation Seat, the mycelium chair that grows into a cast of your habits, the Vertical Rest that abandons lowering your center of gravity entirely, and the Inherited Posture that makes sitting an archive of dead strangers' bodies. That last idea is the single strangest swing across all three responses and still coheres. B is close and equally crafted, with The Understudy (chair as a temporary tutor that hands off a posture profile and disappears) being the freshest single concept, but it overlaps with A on the Debt, grown-chair, and multi-body social-physics moves while landing them slightly less viscerally. C is the most compressed and least developed; its Absence Chair and Conversational Chair echo A's and B's stronger versions in thinner, less vivid form, so its craft and originality lag.
